text.title: SQL Optimization Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of SQL Optimization. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Index added 'because it might help'; no measurable improvement; index later dropped.
antipattern.fix: EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) before AND after every change; record both in the report.

antipattern.symptom: Planner picks nested-loop on 1M rows; new index does nothing.
antipattern.root-cause: Estimate=10, actual=1M; planner picked wrong plan.
antipattern.fix: Run ANALYZE on the table; re-EXPLAIN; if still skewed, raise statistics target on the column.

antipattern.symptom: Composite index on (created_at, tenant_id); tenant_id equality query does seq scan.
antipattern.root-cause: Range column first; equality column last.
antipattern.fix: Reorder to (tenant_id, created_at); confirm with EXPLAIN that index scan picks tenant_id prefix.

antipattern.symptom: List query takes 8s for 200 rows; pg_stat shows 200 sub-query executions.
antipattern.fix: Rewrite as JOIN with GROUP BY + aggregate; verify single execution in EXPLAIN.
